result:
Pushed. Here's what was wrong and what was fixed:

**Root causes:**

1. **`staleWhileRevalidate` could reject**: When a `/heap/[id]` page had never been visited (no cache entry) and we're offline, `cached ?? fetchPromise` returned the rejected fetch promise. `respondWith` with a rejected promise → browser shows "This site can't be reached".

2. **RSC requests were completely skipped**: Next.js `<Link>` navigations send RSC fetch requests (`RSC: 1` header). The old SW did `return` without calling `respondWith`, so the browser made a real network request → failed offline → potentially triggering a hard navigation that hit case #1.

**Fixes:**

| Change | Effect |
|---|---|
| `staleWhileRevalidate` resolves `fetchPromise` to `null` on error, then falls back to `/offline.html` | No more rejected promises → no "site can't be reached" |
| Added `/public/offline.html` | Dark-themed "No Signal, Sailor" page with back button |
| Pre-cached `offline.html` during SW install | Always available as fallback, even on very first offline use |
| RSC requests (`RSC: 1`) now handled via `networkFirst` | Gets cached on first online visit; returns 503 (not a network error) offline |
| Cache version bumped to `v2` | Old stale caches cleaned up on activation |
